summary: Completed frontend-only validation of Graph Visualizer and Sorting restoration on public URL. Core graph creation, edge flows (directed/weighted), algorithm runs/validations, timeline/playback controls, and sorting comparison/playback are working. Found one UX bug impacting drag reliability when grabbing node labels.

ui_bugs.issue: Dragging can fail when pointer starts over node text label because the <text> layer intercepts pointer events; drag works only when grabbing non-label circle area.

action_items: Set pointer-events: none on node label text (or route drag mousedown from parent g) so node dragging is reliable from the full node surface.
Re-run quick UI retest for node dragging and edge creation after drag fix.
critical_code_review_comments: GraphCanvas binds drag start only on <circle>, while sibling <text> overlays center area and captures pointer events, causing intermittent drag failures.
Algorithm applicability guards are correctly enforced in GraphPage (topo directed-only, MST undirected-only) and verified via toast behavior when runs are idle.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated on https://visualgo-pro.preview.emergentagent.com using data-testid selectors. Confirmed: canvas node creation, drag (works when grabbing circle area), edge mode creation with weight prompt, weighted toggle labels, directed arrow markers, node/edge delete, start/target tags, random graph generation, BFS/DFS/Dijkstra/Topo/Cycle/Prim/Kruskal runs and applicability toasts, stats/output/pseudocode/step tracker/timeline/playback controls, and sorting comparison + playback controls. Remaining reproducible issue is drag reliability when pointer starts over node label text.
rca of the issue: Repro: add node(s) on /graph, attempt drag starting from node label center. Drag often does not start; starting drag from circle edge works. Root cause: node label <text> element overlays the center and intercepts pointer events, while onMouseDown is attached only to <circle>. Mitigation: add pointer-events: none to label text or move drag-start handler to parent <g> with target filtering.
